commit.message:
Feature/generation optimizations (#729)

* perf: optimize code generation pipeline

- Cache PropertyDeclarations ordering (avoids re-sorting on every access)
- Use StringComparer.Ordinal on 13 Dictionary<string,...> fields across
  JsonSchemaRegistry, JsonSchemaTypeBuilder, TypeDeclaration,
  PropertyDeclaration, VocabularyRegistry, DocumentResolvers, and
  CodeGenerator (avoids culture-aware comparison overhead on URIs/identifiers)
- Cache OrderedSubschemaTypeDeclarations on TypeDeclaration to avoid
  repeated LINQ OrderBy + ToString() allocations in recursive traversals
  (SetNames, MarkNonGeneratedTypes)
- Pre-compute indent strings for levels 0-19 in CodeGenerator, replacing
  a per-call loop with a single cached string Append

Combined with downstream Corvus.Text.Json.CodeGeneration optimizations,
measured speedup: SimpleObject 1.17x, ComplexObject 1.89x,
CompositionType 1.85x (BenchmarkDotNet, net10.0).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* perf: add explicit 2/3/4-arg overloads and params ReadOnlySpan for Append methods

Add non-allocating overloads for AppendLineIndent, AppendIndent, and AppendLine:
- Explicit 2, 3, 4 Segment parameter overloads (all TFMs) to avoid params array allocation
- params ReadOnlySpan<Segment> on net9.0+ for 5+ args (stack-allocated via C# 13)
- params Segment[] retained on netstandard2.0/net8.0 for 5+ args

~460 multi-arg calls per type generation now avoid heap-allocating a Segment[].
Measured ~1-2% memory reduction and consistent throughput improvement.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* perf: add explicit 2/3/4-arg overloads and params ReadOnlySpan for Append methods

Add non-allocating overloads for AppendLineIndent, AppendIndent, and AppendLine:
- Explicit 2, 3, 4 Segment parameter overloads (all TFMs) to avoid params array allocation
- params ReadOnlySpan<Segment> on net9.0+ for 5+ args (stack-allocated via C# 13)
- params Segment[] retained on netstandard2.0/net8.0 for 5+ args

~460 multi-arg calls per type generation now avoid heap-allocating a Segment[].
Measured ~1-2% memory reduction and consistent throughput improvement.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Initial commit: Dimensional Breach movement prototype

Custom kinematic FPS controller built from scratch (no UFPS).
Unity 6000.3.11f1 / URP.

Movement systems implemented:
- Walk, sprint, air control, coyote time, jump buffering
- Slide (capsule resize, friction, camera lower)
- Crouch (62% height, 50% speed, ceiling-aware)
- Wall-run (gravity, max-up-speed cap, 0.8s cooldown)
- Grapple hook (ground + air, 55u range, rope visual, hit marker)
- Jetpack hover (hold Space while airborne after release, 5s energy)
- Double air jump

Tooling:
- IMGUI debug overlay (Tab toggle controls, sensitivity []/], jetpack bar, crosshair)
- 3 tunable movement presets (Balanced / Snappy / Aggressive)
- Traversal trial timer + checkpoint system
- Editor bootstrap (Tools > Dimension Breach > Bootstrap)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix falsy enum values returning None instead of name

AlarmReason.NO_ALARM has value 0, making it falsy in Python. The
_enum_to_lowercase function used 'if enum_value' which incorrectly
returned None for NO_ALARM. Changed to 'if enum_value is not None'
to properly handle zero-valued Flag enums.

This caused battery_monitor alarm and dc_energy_meter alarm to show
as 'unknown' instead of 'no_alarm' in Home Assistant.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
perf: batch generic-assay-meta requests by profile suffix (#5430)

* perf: batch generic-assay-meta requests by profile suffix

Reduce ~100 generic-assay-meta API requests to ~3-5 when querying
multiple studies:

- Rewrite fetchGenericAssayMetaByMolecularProfileIdsGroupByMolecularProfileId
  to group profiles by suffix and make one API call per unique suffix,
  then map results back to individual profile IDs
- Derive genericAssayEntitiesGroupedByGenericAssayType from per-profile
  data in ResultsViewPageStore instead of making separate API calls
- Remove unused import of fetchGenericAssayMetaByMolecularProfileIdsGroupedByGenericAssayType

* Update src/shared/lib/GenericAssayUtils/GenericAssayCommonUtils.ts

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: simplify genericAssayEntitiesGroupedByGenericAssayType to use backend dedup

Let fetchGenericAssayMetaByMolecularProfileIdsGroupedByGenericAssayType
handle deduplication on the backend instead of deriving and deduplicating
client-side from per-profile data.

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
refactor: split oversized test files into per-module test files (#67)

Split 3 large test files (1107, 944, 824 LOC) into smaller focused
files, each under 400 LOC:

hierarchical_memory/tests.rs → tests/{mod,storage,search,statistics,
  consolidation,pattern}_tests.rs

cognitive_memory/tests.rs → tests/{mod,sensory,working,episodic,
  semantic,procedural,prospective,integration}_tests.rs

security/tests.rs → tests/{mod,query_validator,credential_scrubber,
  scope_enforcer,integration}_tests.rs

All 490 tests continue to pass. No logic changes.

* test: add property-based tests with proptest

Add 10 property-based tests covering:
- Experience roundtrip (fields preserved after construction)
- Similarity symmetry (sim(a,b) == sim(b,a))
- Similarity self-identity (sim(a,a) == 1.0)
- Similarity bounded [0.0, 1.0]
- Tag similarity symmetry
- Entity extraction idempotency
- Contradiction detection symmetry
- Invalid confidence rejection
- Experience serde roundtrip (to_map/from_map)
- Composite similarity bounded [0.0, 1.0]

commit.message:
test: add Python integration tests for PyO3 bindings (#61)

* chore: extend .gitignore with Rust, editor, and Python entries

Add missing patterns:
- *~ (editor temp files)
- *.rs.bk (Rust backup files)
- .python-version

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* test: add Python integration tests for PyO3 bindings

Add tests/python/test_bindings.py with 15 integration tests covering:
- PyExperience creation and repr
- PyMemoryConnector store/search and path traversal rejection
- PyGraphStore nodes, edges, search, and count
- Utility functions (entities, similarity, contradiction, scrub)
- PySecureMemoryBackend creation
- PyCognitiveMemory store/recall

Tests skip gracefully when amplihack_memory_rs module is not built
(requires maturin). CI already picks these up via 'pytest tests/'.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Update to .NET 10 SDK with workload version set pinning (#3514)

Upgrade SkiaSharp from .NET 8 SDK to .NET 10 SDK with comprehensive build system,
CI pipeline, TFM architecture, and workload management changes.

TFM Architecture:
- Restructured from Previous/Current/Next to Base/Previous/Current
- TFMBase=net6.0 (class libraries), TFMPrevious=net9.0, TFMCurrent=net10.0
- Dropped TFMNext (platform TFMs only support 2 versions at once)
- Added Previous TPVs for all platforms alongside Current TPVs

SDK & Tooling:
- global.json: SDK 8.0.100 -> 10.0.100, rollForward latestMinor -> latestFeature
- latestFeature allows any 10.0.xxx feature band across heterogeneous CI agents
- .NET SDK pipeline variable: 8.0.304 -> 10.0.103
- Xcode: 15.4 -> 26.2 (managed), 15.4 -> 16.2 (native)
- Docker images: dotnet/sdk:8.0 -> dotnet/sdk:10.0
- Emscripten: 3.1.34 -> 3.1.56
- macOS agents: macos-14 -> macos-15
- Android tests moved from macOS to Linux agents

Workload Management:
- Replaced ad-hoc manifest mode with deterministic workload version set pinning
- Official workloads install with 'dotnet workload install --version 10.0.104'
- Tizen installs separately (not part of official workload sets)
- Removed DOTNET_VERSION_PREVIEW, DOTNET_WORKLOAD_SOURCE, installPreviewVs
- Removed manifest mode workarounds (--skip-manifest-update)

Platform Versions (Previous / Current):
- Android: 35.0 / 36.0
- iOS: 18.0 / 26.2
- Mac Catalyst: 18.0 / 26.2
- tvOS: 18.0 / 26.2
- macOS: 15.0 / 26.2
- Tizen: 8.0 / 10.0

Package Updates:
- xunit: 2.4.2 -> 2.9.3
- xharness CLI: 9.0.0-prerelease.24317.3 -> 11.0.0-prerelease.26107.1
- DeviceRunners: 0.1.0-preview.1 -> 0.1.0-preview.6
- TypeScript MSBuild: 5.7.1 -> 5.9.3
- Uno.Sdk: 5.2.175 -> 6.5.31

Blazor TypeScript Fix:
- Replaced TypeScriptCompileBlocked with per-TFM TypeScriptOutDir to fix
   multi-TFM parallel build race condition on shared wwwroot/*.js files

CI Cleanup:
- Removed old NuGet feeds (dotnet6/7/8, test-device-runners) from nuget.config
- Fixed Android emulator XDG path mismatch on Linux via ANDROID_AVD_HOME
- Added ANDROID_PLATFORM_VERSIONS: 21,35,36

Samples:
- MAUI: Removed Tizen platform folder, removed UseMauiCompatibility
- Uno: Updated to 6.5, migrated to UnoPlatformHostBuilder
- All: Updated TFMs and SupportedOSPlatformVersion minimums

Documentation:
- New: documentation/updating-dotnet-version.md for future upgrades

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: SkiaSharp
repository.description: SkiaSharp is a cross-platform 2D graphics API for .NET platforms based on Google's Skia Graphics Library. It provides a comprehensive 2D API that can be used across mobile, server and desktop models to render images.

commit.message:
fix: isolate sub-agent output from main chat and render in card

Sub-agent assistant text and reasoning were leaking into the main chat
transcript. Route sub-agent streaming deltas and final messages into
per-subagent accumulators keyed by tool call ID, using ParentToolCallId
from the SDK with state-based fallback. Persist transcript/reasoning in
the sub-agent payload JSON for rebuild, and update SubagentToolCallItem
directly via TranscriptBuilder for immediate UI visibility inside the
expandable sub-agent card.

Files changed:
- ChatViewModel.SessionLifecycle.cs: sub-agent stream routing and state
- ChatViewModel.cs: BuildSubagentPayloadJson transcript/reasoning fields
- TranscriptBuilder.cs: UpdateSubagentTranscript/ReasoningText methods
- TranscriptItems.cs: TranscriptText/ReasoningText properties on card
- TranscriptPaging.cs: weight estimation for sub-agent card content
- ChatView.axaml: render reasoning + transcript inside sub-agent card
- ChatView.axaml.cs: treat active sub-agent cards as streaming content

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- Qualcomm_Financial_Model_Report.pdf: generated output (~40+ pages)
- Cover page with brand design, header/footer on all content pages
- Executive summary dashboard, clickable TOC, PDF bookmarks
- All financial data hardcoded (FY2020-FY2025 historical + Y1-Y7 projections)
- 6 embedded matplotlib charts (revenue, FCFF, DCF bridge, tornado, S-curve, pie)
- All 34 sections: Prompts 0-22C (model) + Prompts 23-31 (strategy) + risks + thesis
- Alternating row colors, professional Qualcomm brand blue theme

commit.message:
test: add property-based tests with fast-check for scheduler invariants

Introduces randomized stress-testing of the scheduling engine using fast-check.
Each test generates random inputs (teachers, subjects, quotas, seeds) and
verifies key invariants hold across 50+ randomized runs.

Invariants tested:
- Structural integrity (cells are null or string)
- Schedule dimensions match configuration
- No teacher teaches two classes at the same time slot
- Lab sessions always occupy adjacent pairs (no orphans)
- Lab room not double-booked across classes
- Main subject quotas not exceeded
- Determinism (same seed → same schedule)
- schedulerIsFullyValid() reports no critical violations

Discovery: lab-across-lunch can occur under tight constraints (documented
as known scheduler limitation, tracked statistically).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(recall): add WITH clause between YIELD and WHERE for KuzuDB compatibility

KuzuDB's CALL...YIELD syntax does not support a WHERE clause directly
after YIELD. A WITH clause must be inserted between YIELD and WHERE.

Fixes scoped recall queries for both vector (QUERY_VECTOR_INDEX) and
full-text (QUERY_FTS_INDEX) search paths.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Implement Notezilla features: auth, note CRUD, authorization, tests, Postgres, deployment config

- Added delete notes with confirmation UI
- Implemented show note with dedicated view page
- Configured email/password authentication via Laravel Breeze
- Created clean landing page with call-to-action
- Updated branding to Notezilla throughout
- Migrated database from SQLite to PostgreSQL
- Set up separate test database with Podman
- Configured Laravel Cloud deployment
- Rewrote README with setup and deployment instructions
- Wrote comprehensive feature tests (12 tests for notes CRUD/auth)
- Wrote unit tests for models (5 tests)
- Created authorization policies for note access control
- All 42 tests passing with 105 assertions
- Fixed migrations for Postgres compatibility

Features:
- Note creation, viewing, editing, and deletion
- User authentication with registration and login
- Authorization policies prevent users from accessing other users' notes
- Guest users redirected to login for protected routes
- Production-ready environment configuration
- Comprehensive test coverage

commit.message:
Implement all requested features: authentication, delete, show, and landing page

- Add user_id foreign key to notes table with migration
- Implement auth middleware on note routes to protect them
- Implement show() and destroy() controller methods with proper logic
- Fix edit() method bug (was referencing wrong variable)
- Update index() to show only authenticated user's notes
- Update store() to associate notes with authenticated user
- Create show.blade.php view with delete confirmation
- Update index view with edit and delete buttons
- Update create and edit views to use Breeze layout
- Install and configure Laravel Breeze for email/password authentication
- Add navigation link to notes in Breeze navigation
- Create clean landing page (welcome.blade.php) for guests with CTA
- Landing page shows different UI for authenticated vs guest users
- Add confirmation dialog on note deletion (no accidental deletes)

All changes follow existing codebase conventions using Tailwind CSS, Blade templating, and Eloquent ORM.
